notes: A push-stale-commits ActionPlan executes successfully (git push returns 0) but the post-execution postcondition that origin/<branch> SHA equals the local <branch> SHA fails — origin still points at the old SHA because of a race with another agent's force-push that arrived between the local commit and our push. Per plan.md §8.3.1 the daemon rolls back the action's cached state ('branch-up-to-date' flag), refuses to mark the bead as pushed, emits an activity warning, and the audit log records the precondition pass + postcondition fail + rollback decision regardless of [SILENT] semantics (Guardrail 10). wakeAgent stays false because the deterministic rollback covers the recovery path; an LLM reach is unnecessary.
